description: Iraq invades Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War and fears of a major supply disruption from the region.
Currency collapse in Thailand spreads across East Asia, sharply reducing regional oil demand expectations.

event_name: OPEC production dispute and price collapse
description: OPEC members fail to agree on effective output cuts amid oversupply, pushing Brent to historic lows (~$10/barrel in Dec 1998).
Terrorist attacks in the US trigger a global demand shock and risk aversion, with short-term price volatility.
Invasion of Iraq raises concerns over supply disruption from a major OPEC producer.
Prices peak amid strong global demand and speculative activity just before the global financial crisis.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggers a global recession, collapsing oil demand and prices within months.

event_name: Libyan Civil War / Arab Spring disruption
description: Civil war in Libya removes significant OPEC light-sweet crude supply from the market, tightening global supply.
Rapid growth in US shale production adds substantial new global supply, pressuring prices downward through 2014.

event_name: OPEC decision not to cut production
description: OPEC opts to maintain output despite falling prices, accelerating the 2014-2016 price collapse (Brent falls below $30 by Jan 2016).

event_name: OPEC+ agreement on coordinated production cuts
description: OPEC and non-OPEC producers (led by Russia) agree to the first coordinated output cuts since 2008, supporting a price recovery.

event_name: US withdrawal from Iran nuclear deal / renewed sanctions
description: US reimposes sanctions on Iranian oil exports, removing supply from the market and lifting prices.

event_name: Attack on Saudi Aramco Abqaiq facility
description: Drone/missile strikes temporarily knock out roughly half of Saudi Arabia's oil production capacity.
OPEC+ talks collapse; Saudi Arabia launches a price war and floods the market with supply just as COVID-19 demand collapses.

event_name: OPEC+ record production cut agreement (COVID-19 response)
description: OPEC+ agrees to historic ~9.7 million bpd output cuts to stabilize prices amid pandemic-driven demand collapse.
Russia's invasion of Ukraine triggers Western sanctions on Russian energy exports and major supply-security fears.
